growth_records.cite: Lever et al. Archaeal Hel308 suppresses recombination through a catalytic switch that controls DNA annealing. Nucleic Acids Research (2023). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkad572. [PMC10484726]
growth_records.snippet: We observed that Delta-hel308 cells have a doubling time of 5.25 h, compared to 2.75 h for wild type cells (Figure 4B).
growth_records.notes: mu = ln2/Td using the reported doubling time of 2.75 h for wild-type H. volcanii H26 grown in Hv-YPC broth, 150 uL cultures in 96-well microtiter plates, 45C, double orbital shaking 807 rpm; generation time calculated by the authors from OD600 growth curves (OD600 0.08-0.16) using G=t/n, n=(log b - log B)/log 2.

growth_records.cite: Lever et al. Archaeal Hel308 suppresses recombination through a catalytic switch that controls DNA annealing. Nucleic Acids Research (2023). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkad572. [PMC10484726]
growth_records.snippet: We observed that Delta-hel308 cells have a doubling time of 5.25 h, compared to 2.75 h for wild type cells (Figure 4B).
growth_records.notes: mu = ln2/Td using the reported doubling time of 5.25 h for Delta-hel308 H. volcanii cells, same Hv-YPC broth/96-well microtiter/45C/807rpm assay as the paired wild-type record.

growth_records.cite: Lever et al. Archaeal Hel308 suppresses recombination through a catalytic switch that controls DNA annealing. Nucleic Acids Research (2023). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkad572. [PMC10484726]
growth_records.snippet: Comparing growth rates (Figure 5D) showed similar generation times for wild type hel308+ and hel308 F316A mutant cells (4 and 5h, respectively)
growth_records.notes: mu = ln2/Td using the reported generation time of 4 h for wild-type H. volcanii in the Figure 5D experiment (same Hv-YPC broth/96-well/45C assay as Figure 4B, a separate replicate set paired with the F316A and D145N mutant comparisons).

growth_records.cite: Lever et al. Archaeal Hel308 suppresses recombination through a catalytic switch that controls DNA annealing. Nucleic Acids Research (2023). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkad572. [PMC10484726]
growth_records.snippet: Comparing growth rates (Figure 5D) showed similar generation times for wild type hel308+ and hel308 F316A mutant cells (4 and 5h, respectively)
growth_records.notes: mu = ln2/Td using the reported generation time of 5 h for the hel308-F316A mutant, same Hv-YPC broth/96-well microtiter/45C assay.

growth_records.cite: Lever et al. Archaeal Hel308 suppresses recombination through a catalytic switch that controls DNA annealing. Nucleic Acids Research (2023). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkad572. [PMC10484726]
growth_records.snippet: the generation time of the hel308 D145N/F316A double mutant was similar to hel308 D145N (10 and 11h, respectively)
growth_records.notes: mu = ln2/Td using the reported generation time of 10 h for the hel308-D145N point mutant, same Hv-YPC broth/96-well microtiter/45C assay.

growth_records.cite: Lever et al. Archaeal Hel308 suppresses recombination through a catalytic switch that controls DNA annealing. Nucleic Acids Research (2023). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkad572. [PMC10484726]
growth_records.snippet: the generation time of the hel308 D145N/F316A double mutant was similar to hel308 D145N (10 and 11h, respectively)
growth_records.notes: mu = ln2/Td using the reported generation time of 11 h for the hel308-D145N/F316A double mutant, same Hv-YPC broth/96-well microtiter/45C assay.

growth_records.cite: Blombach et al. Structural and functional adaptation of Haloferax volcanii TFEα/β. Nucleic Acids Research (2018). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkx1302. [PMC5861453]
growth_records.snippet: the Hvo ΔtfeB strain H2644 proved to be viable but showed a severe growth phenotype, with a doubling time of 5.6 hrs compared to 2.2 h for the parental strain H53 (Figure 5A and B).
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (2.2 h) directly reported in text for parental strain H53 grown in Hv-YPC broth at 45°C in 48-well plates with double-orbital shaking (425 rpm), monitored by OD650 (BioTek Epoch2 microplate spectrophotometer). growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln(2)/doubling_time = 0.6931/2.2 = 0.315 h^-1. Oxygen condition not explicitly stated in text (left null); H. volcanii is a known obligate aerobe but this was not asserted by the paper itself. pH of growth medium not stated (pH 7.5/8.0 values in paper refer to buffers used in other procedures, not the growth medium). [metadata completed from paper Methods: oxygen]

growth_records.cite: Blombach et al. Structural and functional adaptation of Haloferax volcanii TFEα/β. Nucleic Acids Research (2018). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkx1302. [PMC5861453]
growth_records.snippet: the Hvo ΔtfeB strain H2644 proved to be viable but showed a severe growth phenotype, with a doubling time of 5.6 hrs compared to 2.2 h for the parental strain H53 (Figure 5A and B).
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (5.6 h) directly reported in text for ΔtfeB deletion mutant H2644 (independently-derived mutant H2645 gave identical results) grown in Hv-YPC broth at 45°C in 48-well plates with double-orbital shaking (425 rpm), monitored by OD650. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln(2)/doubling_time = 0.6931/5.6 = 0.1238 h^-1. Oxygen condition not explicitly stated in text (left null). pH of growth medium not stated. [metadata completed from paper Methods: oxygen]
